headline: Ferran Torres lifts Spain past 10-man Argentina in extra time
summaryVi: Tây Ban Nha thắng Argentina 1-0 sau hiệp phụ để giành chức vô địch World Cup lần thứ hai. Enzo Fernandez nhận thẻ vàng thứ hai ở phút bù giờ cuối hiệp hai, trước khi Ferran Torres ghi bàn quyết định phút 106. Argentina không thể bảo vệ ngôi vương; Lionel Messi khép lại giải với vị trí á quân.

decisiveEvents.detail: Second yellow card for a foul on Pau Cubarsi

bigMoments: Argentina held Spain scoreless for 90 minutes but entered extra time with ten players after Enzo Fernandez's dismissal.
Substitute Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute to decide a tense final.
Spain completed a tournament built on control, defensive structure and squad depth, winning their second men's World Cup title after 2010.
tournamentImpact: Spain are FIFA Men's World Cup champions for the second time.
Argentina finish runners-up and fail to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to retain the trophy.
The 48-team tournament concludes after 104 matches across Canada, Mexico and the United States.

standoutPlayers.name: Ferran Torres
standoutPlayers.reason: Scored the extra-time winner as a substitute.

standoutPlayers.name: Rodri
standoutPlayers.reason: Anchored Spain's control and passing structure throughout the tournament.

standoutPlayers.name: Lionel Messi
standoutPlayers.reason: Led Argentina to another final but could not produce a final late escape.

focusTeams.Argentina: Runners-up after a 1-0 extra-time defeat; the title defence ended after Enzo Fernandez's dismissal changed the final.
focusTeams.Brazil: Previously eliminated by Norway; no new match-day change.
focusTeams.Portugal: Previously eliminated by Spain; Spain's title confirms they were beaten by the eventual champions.
